pi_items.source: Official badminton court regulations: 13.4m (44 feet) length x 6.1m (20 feet) width for doubles play. Web searches from olympics.com, sportsvenuecalculator.com, and other authoritative badminton sources confirm these exact dimensions in millimeters.

Verified by calculation: 13400mm total court width ÷ 2 = 6700mm. This represents the center of the court where the net is positioned, confirmed by multiple sources stating the net divides the court into two equal 22-foot (6.7m) halves.

pi_items.metadata_value: Y=460 and Y=5640 (460mm from each edge)
pi_items.source: Verified from badminton regulations: singles court is 5.18m (17 feet) wide, doubles court is 6.1m (20 feet) wide. The difference of 0.92m is split equally on each side: 0.46m = 460mm per side. Web sources (olympics.com, jackwinsafety.com, sportsgeeks.net) confirm the alley width is 1.5 feet (0.46m) on each side.

Verified from badminton regulations: short service line is 1.98m (6.5 feet) from the net. With net at X=6700: 6700 - 1980 = 4720 and 6700 + 1980 = 8680. Multiple authoritative sources (olympics.com, jackwinsafety.com, dimensions.com) confirm the 1.98m measurement.

Verified from badminton doubles regulations: long service line is 0.76m (2.5 feet) inward from the baseline. 0.76m = 760mm. Web sources (olympics.com, sportsvenuecalculator.com, jackwinsafety.com) confirm this measurement for doubles courts.
Verified by calculation: 6100mm total court height ÷ 2 = 3050mm. The center line divides the service courts into left and right service areas.
Task description specifies these three layers. Verifier.py checks for these exact layer names (case-insensitive). This is a logical organization for a CAD badminton court design.
Confirmed by setup_task.sh and verifier.py which both reference this exact path. This is the standard output location for LibreCAD user files.

privileged_info_summary: The badminton court task uses accurate regulation doubles court dimensions: 13,400mm length × 6,100mm width. All line positions are mathematically correct based on official badminton court standards: singles sidelines are 460mm from each edge, short service lines are 1,980mm from the net (at X=4720 and X=8680), long service lines are 760mm from baselines (at X=760 and X=12640), the net is positioned at the court center (X=6700), and the center line runs at the court midpoint (Y=3050). The three required layers (Court_Lines, Net_Line, Annotations) follow standard CAD practice. The output file path is consistent across all setup and verification scripts.
